subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, OECD, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Peruvian official (INEI, Banco Central de Reserva del Peru) + Peruvian/Spanish-language press (El Comercio, La Republica, IDL-Reporteros).
A genuinely two-sided profile: a credible, institutionally-anchored macro core sits against chronic political dysfunction (a revolving door of presidents) and a deteriorating security environment.

Standout drags: chronic political instability, weak rule of law, and a sharp crime wave; standout strengths: a standout-credible central bank, low debt, low inflation, and an open economy. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone; Spanish investigative reporting under-read.

economic.notes: A standout-credible central bank, low debt, and low inflation - a genuine macro bright spot despite political chaos.

fiscal_state.notes: Low public debt (~33% GDP), a prudent fiscal tradition, and investment grade - a genuine strength, with some fiscal-rule slippage and unfunded congressional bills flagged. (IMF Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: An autonomous, highly credible central bank with neutral-rate discipline - a standout strength. (BCRP; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The sol is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Long-anchored in the 1-3% target band, with transitory 2026 supply shocks fading. (BCRP)

banking_stability.notes: Sound, well-regulated, and well-capitalised, with declining dollarisation. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: World-leading copper plus gold/zinc, with recurrent mining-corridor social conflict; a paradox of plenty partly buffered by macro institutions. (company filings; HRW)

institutional.notes: Credible statistics, against chronic corruption, weak capacity, and a declining press environment.

rule_of_law.notes: Chronic corruption (nearly every recent president investigated or jailed) and weak courts, with some prosecutorial independence remaining. (TI CPI; WJP; HRW)

statistical_integrity.notes: The statistics agency and central bank are credible and trusted - a relative strength. (IMF; BCRP)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Unstable, politicised, with low delivery capacity. (OECD)

press_freedom.notes: Sharply declining (RSF ~130th), a 2025 'gag law', journalist murders, and judicial harassment. (RSF 2025/2026)

political_social.notes: Chronic political dysfunction and deep social cleavages, against lawful migration handling.

political_stability.notes: Chronic dysfunction - a revolving door of presidents and impeachments, the latest installed via the 2025 ouster of a deeply unpopular predecessor, atop a discredited Congress, with a 2026 election ahead. (CFR; Atlantic Council)

civil_liberties.notes: Formal freedoms intact but undercut by the 2023 protest deaths and rights-suspending states of emergency. (HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful routes that absorbed ~1.6M Venezuelans, against rising xenophobia and a 2025 border state of emergency. (UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep Lima-versus-interior, ethnic, and class cleavages, with the 2022-23 unrest legacy. (V-Dem)

demographics.notes: Young-ish, with fertility slowing toward replacement - a modest tailwind. (INEI; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A pragmatic, very open economy, against residual internal narco-violence.

alliance_reliability.notes: A pragmatic, open economy trading with both the US and China (a new China-built mega-port); non-aligned. (Americas Quarterly)

conflict_involvement.notes: No external war, with residual narco-violence in a remote valley and an organized-crime expansion. (HRW)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: A very open capital account, unsanctioned - a long-standing strength. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Seismic and climate exposure, a weak health system, and a sharply deteriorating crime environment.

climate.notes: Tropical-glacier loss (the largest share globally) plus Lima water plus strong-ENSO flooding, low readiness. (IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: A weak, uneven public system with a catastrophic pandemic-mortality legacy. (World Bank; WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Improving (a new port, a metro) but uneven, with large rural gaps. (OECD)

crime_safety.notes: A sharp deterioration - surging extortion and organized crime, with serial states of emergency. (HRW; Atlantic Council)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against high seismic/flood exposure. (World Bank)

summary: A country split between a remarkably credible macro core and a dysfunctional political one. On the strong side: an autonomous, standout-credible central bank, low public debt, low anchored inflation, sound banks, a very open economy, and trusted statistics. On the weak side: chronic political instability (a revolving door of presidents and impeachments, a discredited Congress), weak rule of law with pervasive corruption, a declining press environment, deep Lima-versus-interior cleavages, seismic and climate exposure, and a sharply deteriorating crime wave. The macro strength is real but increasingly hostage to state incapacity.
